content.full_text:
Donald A. Barrier
567 East Lassen Ave. Space 218
Chico, California 95926
Phone: (916) 342-4510


Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

November 15, 1990

Attention: Mr. Steve Rhodes

Dear Mr. Rhodes:

As a private and patriotic citizen I see our democratic elective process
as a wonderful legacy, a right fought for with the blood of our young
and brave since 1776. Voting for someone of our choice is a privilege
only now being extended to countries throughout Eastern Europe who have
suffered for so many years under the oppressive boot of
totalitarianism.

I am 64 years of age. I spent the last of my teen years in the service
of our great and free country during World War II as a Marine in the
South Pacific and China. In China I saw first hand the communist
takeover which merely changed one oppressor (Japan) for another perhaps
even worse, the Mao evil Communist regime.

I take our elective process very seriously and I am very upset by modern
day methods which border on or are over the line on possible illegal
slander. During the last few elections, the last minute hate,hit pieces
are distributed and no one is willing to stand up and claim their
responsibility until forced to do so. This is the case in the Chico City
Council election. The enclosed news articles are pretty self explanatory
and the letters to the editor show just how others feel about the
underhanded methods used by opposition groups. Bear in mind that for
every letter received there are probably thousands who feel the same but
do not take the time or effort to write.

Aren't there laws that make people accountable for their actions
regarding these hate mailers? Don't the parties involved have to declare
by law their contribution for postage and in-kind services on their
respective campaign fund disclosures? Is this disreputable practice
legal and if so what do we do to make it illegal?

We read after every election just how apathetic the eligible voting
public seems to be. With each election worse than the last it is not
hard to understand why people that all candidates are crooked. When we do
get a fine honest man like Ted Hubert to run for office, they are
subjected to the lies from the opposition. Human nature being what it
is, people always seem to remember the bad said about a person whether
it is true or not and as long as people like Mc Ginnis, Dolan,
Mulholland and the Tom Hayden machine are allowed to continue elections
by fraud and deceit, things will only get worse.

It is time to put an end to the corrupt groups like Hayden's Campaign
California who think that they are above our moral and legal laws. Can
you imagine just how many Republican voters were mislead by the phony
voting guide which showed in very small print (hard for us old timers to
read) at the bottom which stated it was printed and distributed by a
supposed group called "Citizens For Republican Values". This phony hit
piece possibly cost Senator Jim Nielsen the election. An election won in
this manner is a shallow victory for all of us.

If this disregard for honesty is allowed to continue we might as well
become what the rest of the world knows is wrong through trial and error
and is throwing over, communism and totalitarianism.

Please peruse the enclosed material and supply me with some answers.


Sincerely

Donald A. Barrier
Donald A. Barrier

sections.question_synthetic: The writer inquires whether there are laws requiring accountability for negative campaign mailers, whether contributors must disclose funding for such materials in campaign disclosures, and whether such practices are legal or should be made illegal.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming citizen request/complaint letter, not an FPPC advice letter response. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present. The document contains the citizen's concerns about campaign ethics and requests for legal guidance rather than FPPC's authoritative response.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: The writer inquires whether there are laws requiring accountability for negative campaign mailers, whether contributors must disclose funding for such materials in campaign disclosures, and whether such practices are legal or should be made illegal.

embedding.summary: A citizen writes to the FPPC expressing concerns about negative campaign tactics used in a Chico City Council election, specifically questioning the legality of anonymous or unaccountable 'hate mailers' and requesting clarification on campaign finance disclosure requirements.
